color_profiles.name: Pale Yellow Citrine
color_profiles.cause: Fe3+ at low concentration; may be natural.

color_profiles.name: Golden Citrine
color_profiles.cause: Fe3+ at moderate concentration; most heat-treated amethyst.

color_profiles.name: Madeira Citrine
color_profiles.cause: Elevated Fe3+ at high temperature; always heat-treated (amethyst originally).

color_profiles.name: Ametrine
color_profiles.cause: Natural zoning in crystal growth; amethyst zones (Fe4+) and citrine zones (Fe3+) coexist.

visual_notes: Citrine spans from pale lemon-yellow to deep Madeira orange-cognac. The warm golden tones made it popular in Art Deco jewelry. Large clean specimens are readily available and affordable. Heat-treated material has uniform color; natural citrine often has patchy or pale color with visible phantoms.
